provenance.author: Gemini Code Assist
provenance.context: A semantic capsule describing agent interaction protocols.

intent.primary_goal: To define the standardized methods of communication between agents, and between agents and players, ensuring predictable and parsable interactions.

identity.role: The API and communication contract for all agents in the Wright Universe.

priorities: Player-facing communication uses natural language routed through the `commands.js` plugin.
Agent-to-agent communication uses structured messages, preferably via `sharedState` event emitters or functions.
All interactions that change world state or agent goals must be auditable via ChronoSCRIBE.
Error states and requests for clarification must follow a defined format.
Consensus-seeking interactions must follow the Leighton-Weighted Consensus Protocol.

file_scope.purpose: To provide a clear set of rules for how agents talk to each other and to players, preventing conversational chaos.

key_concepts.direct_command: A single-word command from a player (e.g., 'build').
key_concepts.natural_intent: A conversational phrase from a player that is parsed into a command.
key_concepts.agent_proposal: A structured message an agent sends to initiate a consensus cycle.
key_concepts.state_update: An event emitted by a plugin to inform other plugins of a change.

trust.note: This capsule is the authoritative summary of all agent communication rules. It is the 'TCP/IP' of the CobbleWright Collective.
